name: Almería Seafront and Andarax River Park Loop
name_original: Almería – running circular por sus parques y paseo marítimo
description: Jog this 5.9-mile (9.5 km) circular route through Almería's parks and along its seafront promenade for varied scenery and moderate exercise.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax traverse a diverse landscape, from the rugged volcanic coastlines of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park to the arid expanses of the Tabernas Desert. The region features sharp peaks, dramatic cliffs, and numerous coves along the coast, alongside dry riverbeds and distinctive rock formations inland. This variety offers a range of terrains for running, including challenging inclines and scenic coastal paths. Best jogging routes around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax The most popular jogging route is Almería Seafront and Andarax River Park Loop, a 6.0 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route offers views of the Almería seafront and follows the Andarax River Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular to Morrón de los Genoveses from San José, a difficult 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route explores the coastal and volcanic terrain of the Cabo de Gata Natural Park. Local runners also love the Salinas de Cabo de Gata – running circular from la Torre de San Miguel, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the coastal salt flats, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax is defined by volcanic coastlines, arid desert landscapes, and dry riverbeds. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal runs to more difficult trail running challenges. The routes in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 80 reviews. More than 6,500 runners have used komoot to explore Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax?
A: There are over 300 running routes in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax, offering a wide range of experiences from coastal paths to desert trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult runs.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on the running trails in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the rugged volcanic coastlines of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park to the arid expanses of the Tabernas Desert. You'll find sharp peaks, dramatic cliffs, and numerous coves along the coast, alongside dry riverbeds and distinctive rock formations inland. Out of over 300 routes, about 30 are considered easy, 184 moderate, and 94 difficult, ensuring options for all abilities.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer scenic coastal views?
A: Yes, many routes provide stunning coastal views, especially within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985282811/parque-natural-de-cabo-de-gata-trail-running-circular-to-morron-de-los-genoveses-from-san-jose" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular to Morrón de los Genoveses from San José&lt;/a&gt; explores the coastal and volcanic terrain, offering breathtaking vistas of the sea and cliffs.

Q: Can I find any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes available. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986453743/salinas-de-cabo-de-gata-running-circular-from-la-torre-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cabo de Gata – running circular from la Torre de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal salt flats. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985270855/la-molata-and-cerro-de-la-hortichuela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Molata and Cerro de la Hortichuela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.7 km trail with varied scenery.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features I can see while running?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. Along your runs, you might encounter the unique desert landscape of the Tabernas Desert, volcanic formations, and pristine beaches. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1065889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sirens' Reef&lt;/a&gt;, and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Genoveses Beach&lt;/a&gt;. For historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2846137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Almería&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are there any longer distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985194977/parque-natural-de-cabo-de-gata-circular-from-agua-amarga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – circular from Agua Amarga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15 km route. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985198069/barranco-del-hondo-mesa-roldan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Hondo - Mesa Roldán Trail&lt;/a&gt;, covering nearly 13.5 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 80 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning and varied natural features, from the volcanic coastlines to the unique desert environments, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes?
A: Yes, there are approximately 30 easy routes in the region. While specific beginner routes are not highlighted here, many of the coastal paths and sections along river parks offer flatter, less challenging terrain suitable for new runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986477968/almeria-seafront-and-andarax-river-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almería Seafront and Andarax River Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with minimal elevation, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Is it possible to find running routes with good parking access?
A: Many routes in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax, especially those starting from towns or popular natural park access points, offer convenient parking. While specific parking details for every route are not listed, the well-developed infrastructure for active tourism suggests that trailheads are generally accessible by car.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax?
A: The region boasts over 3,000 hours of sun annually, making it suitable for running much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the mild climate is inviting. During summer, it's best to run early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the intense midday heat, especially in the more arid desert areas.

Q: Are there any running routes near the city of Almería?
A: Yes, several routes are easily accessible from Almería. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986477968/almeria-seafront-and-andarax-river-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almería Seafront and Andarax River Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering views of the seafront and following the Andarax River. Other options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14606806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Andarax River loop from Universidad de Almería&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40644783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de El Zapillo – Almería Central Market loop from Río Andarax&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running trails that are suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided for all routes, many of the easier and moderate routes with less elevation gain, particularly those along the Almería seafront or within the more accessible parts of the natural park, can be enjoyed by families. Look for routes with well-maintained paths and shorter distances if running with children.
tips: The nice "Parque de la Estación" is located directly behind the train station or as an extension of the same with the original tracks, which are now overgrown by plants and trees. Again in a further extension of the park, the original rail lines run elevated up into the port.

The Almería Seafront Promenade, also known as the Carmen de Burgos Promenade, is an ideal place to enjoy the coast and the Mediterranean atmosphere. This promenade stretches approximately 2.5 kilometers, from the mouth of the Andarax River to the Port of Almería.

This is the largest park in the city of Almería, formerly land dedicated to agriculture that gradually fell into disuse and its use was finally changed to a more recreational one, but kept as green areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 9.5 kilometers (5.9 miles) long and typically takes around 59 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. This duration can vary based on your individual speed and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, while classified as moderate, the route is considered suitable for all skill levels. It features a relatively flat profile with minimal elevation gain, making it accessible for regular joggers and those new to running.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved, ensuring a comfortable jog. Specifically, about 7.8 kilometers are paved, 815 meters are asphalt, and 138 meters are gravel. There's also a small portion of unknown surface type.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Almería running circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy a varied landscape combining urban green spaces with coastal views. Highlights include the Parque de la Estación, the scenic Paseo Marítimo Carmen de Burgos (Almería), and the Parque Periurbano del Andarax, which is one of the city's largest parks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this running route?
faqs.answer: The trail's starting point is easily accessible, and while specific parking areas aren't detailed, its urban nature suggests public parking options would be available nearby in Almería. Consider parking near one of the parks or the promenade.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point of this route by public transport?
faqs.answer: The route is noted for its accessibility, with the starting point easily reachable by public transport. Almería has a local bus network that can connect you to areas near the parks or the seafront promenade.

faqs.question: Is this running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through parks and along the seafront, specific rules for dogs are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in public urban areas and parks, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: Almería enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for running. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended if running during those months. Winter is generally mild.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to use this public running route through Almería's parks and seafront promenade.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, given its moderate difficulty, mostly paved terrain, and passage through parks, this route can be suitable for families looking for an active outing. The varied scenery keeps it interesting.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through urban areas and along the seafront promenade, you will find numerous cafes, restaurants, and public facilities available, especially along the Paseo Marítimo Carmen de Burgos.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. Notably, it covers parts of the Transandalus, Camino del Sauce, Camino de la Borraja, and Camino del Almencino, among others.
